↩️ Reply to OSINT OSINTdefender RT @ianellisjones : My first byline and new carrier tracker for @thewarzonewire ! Introducing TWZ’s weekly carrier tracker monitoring America’s flattop fleet, including deployed Carrier Strike Groups and Amphibious Ready Groups, using publicly available open-source information. Three U.S. Navy Carrier Strike Groups (CSG) have deployed in support of Operation Epic Fury, as two Amphibious Ready Groups (ARG), with Marine Expeditionary Units (MEU) embarked, converge on the Middle East. The George H.W. Bush CSG is the latest to join the fray, departing Naval Station Norfolk on March 31 for a regularly scheduled deployment, reportedly to U.S. Central Command. Bush is set to relieve the Gerald R. Ford CSG, which has been deployed for more than nine months and departed the Port of Split, Croatia, on April 2 following a five-day port call. The Abraham Lincoln CSG is operating from an undisclosed location in the Arabian Sea and conducting nonstop combat operations against Iran. The Tripoli ARG and 31st MEU arrived in CENTCOM last week, and the Boxer ARG and 11th MEU are transiting the Pacific en route to the Middle East. Take a closer look and let me know what you'd like to see at TWZ going forward: https://www.twz.com/sea/carrier-tracker-as-of-april-3-2026 tweet

🇺🇸 ✝️ "Do as little harm as you can, and try to preserve innocent lives." The head of the US Military Archdiocese, the bishop with direct authority over every Catholic chaplain in the armed forces, has declared the Iran war unjust ahead of an interview set to air on CBS this Easter Sunday. Archbishop Broglio, one of the most conservative Catholic prelates in America, said the war "anticipates a nuclear threat rather than responding to realized danger" failing the Church's just war criteria. He told Catholic troops to "do as little harm as you can." He also rebuked Pete Hegseth's invocations of Jesus to justify the war as "problematic," saying it's "hard to cast this war as something that would be sponsored by the Lord." This is the bishop the Trump administration thought they could count on. Letter from Leo. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇩🇪 Berliner Zeitung reports that men in Germany are now required to obtain official permission to leave the country. All males aged 17 to 45 must get approval from a Bundeswehr career center if they plan to stay abroad for more than three months, including for study, work, or long-term travel. The change came into effect largely unnoticed as part of the Military Service Modernization Act. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
